---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

<!--
## What this file does

Loads two pre-computed time-series vectors from `data.RData`:

- `GC_int` — per-frame Lifeact-mScarlet actin intensity at the
  **growth cone / lamellipodium** ROI of Cell_6.
- `soma_int` — per-frame Lifeact-mScarlet intensity at the **soma** ROI
  of the same cell.

The script then:

1. Converts `Time` from frame index to seconds (`Time = Time * 10`,
   reflecting a 10-s frame interval).
2. Reshapes to long format with two `region` levels renamed
   `Lamellipodium` (GC) and `Soma`.
3. Plots both traces on the same axes, with **TWO cyan-shaded vertical
   bands** marking the photoactivation periods (`x = 200–590` and
   `800–1190` seconds — two sequential activations).
4. Saves `soma_int.pdf` (static plot) and `two_groups.gif` (animated
   "reveal" version via `gganimate::transition_reveal`).
5. Re-saves `data.RData` (no analysis change; checkpoint convenience).

The plot has NO statistical test — it is a single-cell representative
trace, not a population-level comparison.

## Manuscript panel mapping

This file feeds **ED Fig 5h** ONLY.

- **ED Fig 5h** (line 353 of docx): **"Temporal profiles of actin
  intensity at the soma and at the lamellipodia. The blue shade marks
  the activation periods."** ← EXACT MATCH to this file's body output:
  two-channel time-course plot of GC vs soma actin intensity with
  cyan/blue-shaded activation bands. Confirmed primary published
  source.

## Source Data

ED Fig 5h's Source Data sheet contains the per-frame `Time`,
`GC_int`, `soma_int` columns for the representative cell. This file's
output `soma_int.pdf` and the underlying long-format dataframe `df_long`
are the published data.

## Note: the original `Cell_6_PatchWaveConvert` working folder name

The original G:\ source path is:
``G:\Figure 3_Arp3\Fig. 3j-n_PARac1\Andor\PARac1\210128_PARac1_LASiR_Myl12amScar\Cell_6_PatchWaveConvert\Neurite activity corr.Rmd``.
The folder name `Cell_6_PatchWaveConvert` and the file name
`Neurite activity corr.Rmd` together imply that Cell_6 was identified
during analysis as the cell whose actin patch → wave conversion was
clearest, and was chosen as the representative for ED Fig 5h. This is
why the file operates on a single cell, not a population.

## Original G:\ source

G:/Figure 3_Arp3/Fig. 3j-n_PARac1/Andor/PARac1/210128_PARac1_LASiR_Myl12amScar/Cell_6_PatchWaveConvert/Neurite activity corr.Rmd
-->

## Package installation

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(pracma)
library(peakPick)
library(quantmod)
library(ggpmisc)
library(tidyverse)
library(magrittr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)
library(forecast)
library(tsibble)
library(broom)
library(tidyquant)
library(tsbox)
library("pheatmap")
library("RColorBrewer")
library(ggpmisc)
library(ggfortify)
library(viridis) # Default Color Maps from 'matplotlib' 
library(hrbrthemes)
library(data.table)
library(superheat)
```

## load existing/saved environment (.RData)
```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
setwd(Path_1)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "data.RData",  sep = "/")
load(file=saved_path)

```

```{r}
colnames(GC_int) <- c("Time", "GC_int")
colnames(soma_int) <- c("Time", "soma_int")

df <- cbind(GC_int, soma_int$soma_int)
colnames(df)[3]<- "soma_int"

 df$Time <- df$Time*10
 
 df_long <- df %>% pivot_longer(cols = c("GC_int", "soma_int"), names_to = "region", values_to = "int") %>% as.data.table()
  df_long$int <-  df_long$int/10
  
  df_long[region == "GC_int"]$region <- "Lamellipodium"
  df_long[region == "soma_int"]$region <- "Soma"
```

```{r}
fmt_mmss <- function(s) {
  s <- as.integer(round(s))
  sprintf("%02d:%02d", s %/% 60, s %% 60)
}
```

```{r message = FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=8}
p <- df_long %>% 
ggplot(aes(x=Time, y=int,color = region)) +
 # geom_rect(aes(xmin=200, xmax=590, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf), fill  = "cyan",  colour = NA) +
#  geom_rect(aes(xmin=800, xmax=1190, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf), fill  = "cyan",  colour = NA) +
   #geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=mean-sd, ymax=mean+sd), fill="lightgrey")+
   geom_line(size=1.5, alpha=1) +
  
  #stat_peaks(colour = "red", span = 21, ignore_threshold = 0.8) +
  #stat_peaks(geom = "text", colour = "red", span = 21, ignore_threshold = 0.8, hjust = 2, angle = 0) +
  #stat_valleys(colour = "lightblue", span = 21, ignore_threshold = 1) +
  #stat_valleys(geom = "text", colour = "blue", span = 21, ignore_threshold = 1, hjust = -0.5, angle = 0) +
  
   annotate("rect",
           xmin = 200, xmax = 590, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "cyan", alpha = 0.25, colour = NA) +
  
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 800, xmax = 1190, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "cyan", alpha = 0.25, colour = NA) +
  
  scale_y_continuous( breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 6), name = "Intensity (a.u.)")+
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),  labels = fmt_mmss, breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 7), name = "Time (mm:ss)") +

  theme_ipsum(grid = F, ticks = T)+ 
  theme_linedraw()+
  scale_colour_manual(
  values = c(Lamellipodium = "black", Soma = "grey"),  # name groups exactly as in df$group
  name = "Region"                             # legend title (optional)
)+
   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),  # x-axis label
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),  # y-axis label
        legend.position = c(0.85, 0.85),
     legend.box.background = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.box.margin     = margin(3, 3, 3, 3), 
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 12), 
        axis.text=element_text(size=16)) #for centering the text
```

```{r}
library(gganimate)
p_anim <- p + transition_reveal(along = Time)

anim <- animate(p_anim, fps = 10,  width = 500, height = 600,
                renderer = gifski_renderer())
anim_save("two_groups.gif", anim)
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  "soma_int.pdf",
  plot = last_plot(),
  device = "pdf")
```

```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "data.RData",  sep = "/")
save.image(file=saved_path)

```
